item.title: Clash at filling station injures two in Rajbari
item.description: At least two people were injured when a clash broke out over queue-jumping at a filling station at Kalukhali upazila in Rajbari on Monday night......

item.title: Body of Bangladeshi returned to BGB after 38 days
item.description: Indian authorities have handed over the body of a Bangladeshi national through the Burimari land port in Lalmonirhat, 38 days after his death......Political commentary Bangladesh

item.title: Girl raped in Habiganj, 1 held
item.description: A man was arrested on a charge of involvement in the rape of an adolescent girl in a tea garden at Madhabpur upazila in Habiganj on Monday evening......

item.title: Several injured in C’nawabganj clash after arbitration
item.description: A number of people were injured in a violent clash between two rival groups of villagers during an arbitration in Chapainawabganj Sadar upazila on Tuesday......

item.title: Coast Guard seizes illegal shrimp spawn
item.description: Members of the Bangladesh Coast Guard seized illegal shrimp spawn worth about Tk 4,57,50,000 during a drive in Munshiganj on Tuesday......

item.title: Holidaymakers rush to return to city after Eid
item.description: Thousands of people across Bangladesh continued their return journey to Dhaka on Tuesday as government offices, banks and insurance institutions reopened after a seven-day Eid holiday......

item.title: DU, police vow vigilance to prevent campus unrest
item.description: The University of Dhaka administration and law enforcement agencies on Tuesday stressed heightened vigilance to prevent any attempt to destabilise the campus......City & Local Guides

item.title: Eid-ul-Fitr celebrated
item.description: Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the most significant religious festivals for Muslims, was celebrated across Bangladesh on Saturday with deep devotion, festivity, and enthusiasm......

item.title: Savar serial killer suspect Samrat dies in custody
item.description: The alleged serial killer Mashiur Rahman Khan Samrat, accused of multiple killings at Savar on the outskirts of the capital, has died in custody after falling ill in jail, police said......

item.title: Offices reopen after Eid, presence thin
item.description: Government offices reopened on Tuesday following a weeklong holiday for Eid-ul-Fitr, but the presence of officials remained low at various offices, including the country’s administrative hub, Bangladesh Secretariat, in the capital Dhaka......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: 10 injured, 5 held as BNP, Jamaat clash at Patgram
item.description: A clash broke out between BNP and Jamaat supporters in Patgram, Lalmonirhat, over a Facebook post criticising Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, leaving at least 10 people from both sides injured in the incident......

item.title: 25 injured in Meherpur BNP factional clashs
item.description: AT LEAST 25 people were injured in a clash between two rival factions of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party over local dominance at Gangni upazila in Meherpur on Monday night......

item.title: Two children, one teenager drown in Barishal, Noakhali pond
item.description: Two children and one teenager drowned in ponds in Barishal and Noakhali respectively......

item.description: A three-day Bishnupriya Manipuri Art Festival 2026 was held at the Manipuri Lalitkala Academy at Kamalganj, Moulvibazar......City & Local Guides

item.title: BMU holds Eid reunion
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University organised its Eid Reunion 2026 on Tuesday, bringing together teachers, doctors, students and staff to exchange greetings after Eid ul-Fitr......

item.title: Caretaker govt key chapter in country’s democratic history: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapan on Tuesday said that non-party caretaker governments represented a significant chapter in the democratic history of Bangladesh......

item.title: HC flags rights concerns over long detention
item.description: The High Court has observed that keeping an accused in prolonged detention without a police report raises human rights concerns......

item.title: IMF review deferred for a quarter, loan disbursement too
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Tuesday said that the next review under the current $5.5 billion loan programme with the International Monetary Fund would be held in July......

item.title: Bangladeshis in Iran safe, says state minister
item.description: State minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam on Tuesday said that Bangladeshis, mostly students, staying in war-hit Iran had so far been safe......

item.title: PM orders Tk 10 lakh loan each for higher studies abroad
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday instructed the Ministry of Expatriates’ Welfare and Overseas Employment to take steps to provide loans of Tk 10 lakh to students intending to pursue higher studies abroad......

item.title: Menon, Inu, Justice Manik granted bail in defamation case
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Tuesday granted bail to Workers Party of Bangladesh president Rashed Khan Menon, Jatiya Samajtantrik Dal - Jasad president Hasanul Haq Inu and former Supreme Court judge Justice AHM Shamsuddin Chowdhury Manik in a defamation case......

item.title: Brahmanbaria YouTuber youth beaten to death
item.description: A social media content creator was beaten to death after his abduction at Kasba upazila of Brahmanbaria on Tuesday afternoon......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Baul programme foiled in Sylhet
item.description: No legal step was taken still Tuesday, two days after a Baul music festival at Bishwanath upazila in Sylhet was foiled by attackers......

item.title: 5 killed as Russian missiles hit across Ukraine
item.description: A barrage of Russian missiles and drones killed five people and wounded more than two dozen across Ukraine on Tuesday in the largest attack in weeks, officials said......

item.title: DGHS closes blood bank, two hospitals in Dhaka
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services on Tuesday began drives against irregularities and mismanagement at private healthcare facilities following a scam in the Doctor’s Care Hospital and Diagnostic Centre in the capital’s Shyamoli area......

item.title: Most road crash patients at NITOR victims of battery-run rickshaw accidents
item.description: Most of the road accident victims brought to the National Institute of Traumatology and Orthopaedic Rehabilitation in the five days starting from March 19, the first day of Eid holiday, met with crashes involving battery-run rickshaws......

item.title: Hegseth first one to speak up in support of Iran war: Trump
item.description: President Trump indicated on Monday that defence secretary Pete Hegseth was the first top administration official to back the US military attacks against Iran, reports thehill.com......

item.title: Iran, Israel trade strikes amid Trump talk of negotiations
item.description: Iran and Israel traded strikes on Tuesday, as the Middle East war showed no sign of de-escalation despite US president Donald Trump signalling ‘very good talks’ to end the three-week conflict......

item.title: Are advertisements truly persuasive?
item.description: THROUGHOUT the day, people encounter numerous advertisements — on billboards, YouTube, television, Facebook and other digital platforms. Yet if asked to recall a specific advertisement, many would struggle to remember even one. This raises an uncomfortable question: are our advertisements truly persuasive, or are they merely loud messages that leave......

item.title: Discriminated ‘subsector’ of agriculture
item.description: WE OFTEN ignore the fact that the livestock and fisheries sector contributes significantly to the economy and, more importantly, to food and nutritional security. However, its importance is undermined by the government’s policymaking bodies and civil society, even though their day starts with at least an egg or two for breakfast and fish and meat for lunch and dinner......Geographic Reference

item.title: Recast through culture
item.description: IN AN increasingly fragmented world, where perception frequently precedes fact, nations are no longer defined solely by their economic indicators or political developments. Culture — subtle yet enduring — has emerged as a decisive force in shaping global narratives. For Bangladesh, this presents not only an opportunity but also a pressing urgency......

item.title: Miscalculation and fear of global fallout
item.description: THERE are moments in history when geopolitics ceases to be merely about power and becomes something far more intricate: an unfolding system of interdependence, perception and risk. The current confrontation between the United States and Iran is one such moment. It is not simply a stand-off. It is a game — structured, strategic and dangerously fragile — where......

item.title: Emergency failures in hospitals must end
item.description: THE deaths of two elderly patients in a fire at Barishal Sher-e-Bangla Medical College Hospital on March 17 once again bring to the fore critical concerns about emergency preparedness and patient safety in hospitals. The fire, which broke out at night in a fourth-floor storeroom containing polyfoam, sheets and other flammable materials, filled parts of the building with......

item.title: Time for authorities to stop transport deaths
item.description: A LONG train of death has marked the Eid-time travel between March 19, when offices closed, and March 23, the last day of the business closure, with more than 25 people having been killed. Accidents continued to take place, with large vehicles ramming into small vehicles from behind or head-on, a truck moving through a shop, a bus ploughing through a crowd and......

item.title: India-US consortium buys IPL Royal Challengers Bengaluru for $1.7bn
item.description: A consortium of investors, including US asset management giant Blackstone, on Tuesday announced the takeover of Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB), one of the founding clubs of Indian Premier League, for nearly $1.8 billion....

item.title: Griezmann to leave Atletico for Orlando at end of season
item.description: Antoine Griezmann will leave Atletico Madrid for MLS side Orlando City at the end of the season, the two clubs announced on Tuesday....

item.title: 'Illustrious' Salah to leave Liverpool at the end of the season
item.description: Mohamed Salah will leave Premier League champions Liverpool at the end of the season, the Egypt forward announced on Tuesday as he started a farewell to what the club said were nine ‘illustrious’ years at Anfield....

item.title: Gatekeeper held over Cumilla train-bus crash that killed 12
item.description: Members of the Rapid Action Battalion have arrested the prime accused in a case filed over a deadly train-bus collision in Cumilla that left 12 people dead....

item.title: Offices, banks resume today after eid holidays
item.description: After a seven-day closure for Eid-ul-Fitr, government offices, banks, and insurance institutions across Bangladesh reopened on Tuesday......Bangladesh news updates
